activities_projects.title: Historiographical Analysis of Tawarikh
activities_projects.objective: Critique the reliability and limitations of medieval court chronicles
steps: Deconstruct the 'Birthright' and 'Gender Distinction' arguments used by medieval chroniclers
Analyze why these authors focused exclusively on urban centers and ignored the rural populace
Evaluate the impact of 'Anticipated Rewards' on the objectivity of historical recording
Write a critique on how the chroniclers' preservation of the 'Ideal Social Order' influenced the Sultan's policies
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the ideological bias and social exclusivity of primary literary sources
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical depth in identifying the intersection of social status and historical narrative (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Gender and Political Legitimacy: The Raziyya Case
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the structural barriers to female sovereignty in the 13th century
steps: Analyze Minhaj-i Siraj’s claim that a queen's rule was 'against the creation of God'
Compare the strategies of Raziyya (defiance) vs. Rudramadevi (masquerade) in asserting authority
Investigate the role of the 'Turkish Nobles' (The Forty) in destabilizing Raziyya’s reign
Debate: 'Was the removal of Raziyya a religious necessity or a political power play by the aristocracy?'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes gender studies with medieval political history to understand systemic inequality
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of the argument regarding the social construction of leadership (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Garrison-Hinterland' Economic Model
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the logistics of military occupation and resource extraction
steps: Define the symbiotic yet parasitic relationship between the 'Garrison Town' and its 'Hinterland'
Analyze the shift from 'tribute' to 'direct revenue collection' under the Khaljis
Explain how the clearing of forests in the Doab served both military (road safety) and economic (peasant expansion) purposes
Evaluate the environmental impact of the Sultanate's expansion into the 'Internal Frontier'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the transition from a militarized outpost to a consolidated territorial state
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical accuracy in explaining the shift in revenue and spatial control (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Circle of Justice: A Structural Critique
activities_projects.objective: Deconstruct the socio-economic philosophy of the Sultanate
steps: Analyze the 'Circle of Justice' as a mechanism for maintaining the status quo
Explain how 'Justice' in this context was synonymous with 'Stability' rather than 'Equality'
Investigate the tension between the Sultan's need for high revenue and the peasant’s survival
Write a thesis on how the 'Circle of Justice' justified the exploitation of the peasantry to fund the military
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically assesses the functionalist view of medieval governance
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to interpret 'Justice' as a tool for imperial sustainability (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fiscal Centralization under Alauddin Khalji
activities_projects.objective: Examine the destruction of local aristocratic power
steps: Research the policy of 'Measurement of Land' and the abolition of local chieftain privileges
Analyze the introduction of 'Kharaj' (50% crop tax) and its impact on rural class structures
Explain the role of the 'Mustakhraj' (special collectors) in ensuring central treasury inflow
Evaluate how Khalji’s fiscal policies transformed local 'Ranas' and 'Rawats' into mere subordinates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the shift from feudal/decentralized to bureaucratic/centralized revenue systems
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in explaining the erosion of local intermediaries' power (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Token Currency: A Failure of Trust
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the monetary experiment of Muhammad Tughluq
materials: Images of copper vs. silver tankas
steps: Explain the economic theory behind Muhammad Tughluq’s 'Token Currency' (representative money)
Investigate why the lack of a 'Royal Mint' monopoly led to mass counterfeiting by 'goldsmiths'
Analyze the loss of state 'Credibility' when the Sultan had to exchange copper for real silver
Compare this 14th-century failure with the modern use of paper currency
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the importance of state control over bullion and the psychology of currency
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical understanding of why the 'Token Currency' was a conceptual success but a practical disaster (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Defense: The Mongol Menace
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the strategic divergence between Khalji and Tughluq
steps: Compare the 'Defensive' strategy of Alauddin (Siri fort/market controls) with the 'Offensive' preparation of Tughluq
Analyze the logistical nightmare of shifting the 'Hinterland' support from Delhi to Daulatabad
Evaluate the 'Price Control' measures of Alauddin as a form of wartime economic mobilization
Assess which Sultan's administrative strain led to the eventual fragmentation of the empire
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically appraises how extreme external threats force radical administrative shifts
activities_projects.evaluation: Strategic depth in comparing military and administrative resilience (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Masjid-i Jami: Architecture of Assertion
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the congregational mosque as a political tool
steps: Research the term 'Sanctuary of the World' (Jahanpanah) and its political significance
Analyze why the Sultans built many mosques specifically in conquered territories
Evaluate the role of the 'Friday Sermon' (Khutba) in legitimizing the Sultan’s name
Investigate the use of 'Spolia' (reused material) in early Delhi architecture as a sign of conquest
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes architecture as a visual and social medium for asserting political sovereignty
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding the mosque's role beyond worship as a symbol of 'protectorship' (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Muqti vs. State: The Struggle for Autonomy
activities_projects.objective: Examine the tension between provincial governors and the central court
materials: Administrative records of the Tughluq era
steps: Investigate why the Sultans prevented 'Iqtas' from becoming hereditary
Analyze the role of 'Accountants' as spies for the central government in provincial audits
Explain how 'distance from Delhi' correlated with the frequency of Muqti rebellions in Bengal and Sindh
Discuss the transition of the 'Iqta' system into the 'Jagir' system of the later period
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the challenges of governing a vast territory with limited communication technology
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity on the mechanisms of central control and provincial resistance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Sayyid-Lodi Fragmented Sovereignty
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the decentralization of power in the 15th century
steps: Analyze why the 'Afghan' Lodi Sultans were seen as 'first among equals' rather than absolute monarchs
Investigate the rise of regional powers (Malwa, Mewar, Vijaynagar) during the decline of the central Sultanate
Explain how Sher Shah Suri’s administrative innovations bridged the gap between the Sultans and the Mughals
Evaluate the Battle of Panipat (1526) not just as a victory of Babur, but as a failure of the Lodi alliance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the cyclical nature of empire: from centralization to fragmentation and back
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical insight into the political landscape of pre-Mughal India (10 marks)
